original_prompt: Please take a look at the file 'team-building-activities.docx', suggest two more activities, and add them to the file.
assessment_reasoning: The prompt clearly identifies the target file and the desired outcome (suggest two more activities and add them to the file), so the agent knows what to open and modify. It lacks minor specifics such as where exactly to insert the activities (append vs. insert at a particular section), formatting expectations, content style/length, or any approval process. These omissions create slight ambiguity but do not prevent reasonable execution of the task.

variations.L1: I have a team-building document—can you help improve it?
variations.L2: Please suggest two more team-building activities and update the file I uploaded with them.
variations.L3: Open the file named "team-building-activities.docx", suggest two additional team-building activities (each with a one-line description), and add them into the document near the end. Keep the existing formatting.
variations.L4: Please take a look at the file "team-building-activities.docx", propose two more team-building activities (title plus one-sentence description each), append them to the end of the document, and save the updated file.
variations.L5: Find the file by calling search_files_by_filename with filename="team-building-activities.docx". Prepare two additional activities (each a title and a one-sentence description). Then call append_to_file with file_id equal to the found file's id and content equal to the two activities (plain text). Return the file_id and the exact text you appended after saving.
